## Plan: Optimize `src/pipeline.ts`

**Only one file needs to change:** `src/pipeline.ts`. Here's the full breakdown:

---

### Root causes & fixes

| Function | Current (broken) complexity | Fix | Optimized complexity |
|---|---|---|---|
| `groupByCategory` | O(nÂ²) â€” `array.find` per insert + `reduce` to recalculate total | `Map<category, group>`, increment `total` directly | O(n) |
| `findDuplicates` | O(nÂ²) â€” nested loop over all pairs | Single pass into `Map<key, Transaction[]>`, filter entries with length > 1 | O(n) |
| `calculateRunningBalance` | O(n Ã— k) â€” filter + re-sort + recalculate balance from zero for each of k positions | Filter once, sort once, single cumulative-sum pass | O(n + k log k) |
| `topSpenders` | O(n Ã— m) + O(mÂ²) bubble sort | Single pass into `Map<userId, {total,count}>`, then native `Array.sort` | O(n + m log m) |
| `searchTransactions` | New `RegExp` created per transaction, linear dedup scan | Compile regex once before the loop, `Set<id>` for O(1) dedup | O(n) |
| `processAll` | No change needed â€” benefits automatically | â€” | â€” |

---

### Why correctness is preserved
- All key formats, comparators, and rounding logic (`Math.round(x * 100) / 100`) are kept byte-for-byte identical to the originals
- `groupByCategory` returns insertion order (same as before)
- `topSpenders` uses `b.totalSpent - a.totalSpent` comparator â†’ same descending order as bubble sort
- `findDuplicates` key format `` `${userId}|${amount}|${date}` `` is identical; `50.0` serializes to `"50"` in both old and new code; triplicates naturally accumulate in the same Map entry
- `searchTransactions` same regex escape pattern and `"i"` flag

---

### Verification
```bash
npm test   # all 28 tests must pass: 22 correctness + 6 performance
```
